0.b1: Mr. Activationist plans to go on a vacation.
0.cn: 行动派先生计划去度假。
0.ai_notes: 'Activationist': a person who takes action to achieve goals; this is a creative term used here to contrast with 'Passivationist'.
0.remarks: 'Activationist': 指为了实现目标而采取行动的人；这是一个创造性的词汇，用于与 'Passivationist' 形成对比。

1.ai_notes: 'takes it': in this context, it means he actually goes on the trip he planned.
1.remarks: 'takes it': 在此语境下，指他真的去进行了他计划的旅行。

2.original: Mr. Passivationist plans a vacation, but he postpones it until next year.
2.b1: Mr. Passivationist plans a vacation, but he waits until next year to go.
2.cn: 被动派先生也计划去度假，但他把计划推迟到了明年。
2.ai_notes: 'Passivationist': a person who tends to delay or avoid taking action; this is a creative term based on the word 'passive'.
2.remarks: 'Passivationist': 指倾向于拖延或避免采取行动的人；这是一个基于 'passive' 一词的创造性词汇。

3.original: Mr. A decides he should attend church regularly.
3.b1: Mr. Activationist decides to go to church every week.
3.cn: 行动派先生决定定期去教堂。
3.ai_notes: 'drop a note': a casual way to say 'write a short letter or message'.
3.remarks: 'drop a note': 一种随意的说法，意为“写一封简短的信或信息”。

4.ai_notes: 'He does': a short, emphatic way to confirm that he followed through on his plan.
4.remarks: 'He does': 一种简短且强调的说法，确认他确实执行了他的计划。

5.original: Mr. P thinks it's a good idea to go to church regularly,
5.b1: Mr. Passivationist also thinks it is good to go to church regularly,
5.cn: 被动派先生也觉得定期去教堂是个好主意，
5.ai_notes: 'thinks it's a good idea': a common way to express agreement with a plan without necessarily committing to it.
5.remarks: 'thinks it's a good idea': 一种表达赞同某项计划但不一定承诺去做的常用方式。

6.original: too, but he finds ways to postpone acquiring this new habit.
6.b1: but he finds excuses to delay starting this habit.
6.cn: 但他总能找到借口推迟养成这个习惯。
6.ai_notes: 'postpone': to delay something or move it to a later time.
6.remarks: 'postpone': 推迟某事或将其移至稍后的时间。

7.original: Mr. A feels that he should drop a note to someone he knows to congratulate him on some achievement.
7.b1: Mr. Activationist feels he should write a note to congratulate a friend on their success.
7.cn: 行动派先生觉得他应该写张便条，祝贺朋友取得的成就。
7.ai_notes: 'drop a note': a casual way to say 'write a short letter or message'.
7.remarks: 'drop a note': 一种随意的说法，意为“写一封简短的信或信息”。

8.ai_notes: 'He writes the note': a direct statement showing immediate action.
8.remarks: 'He writes the note': 一种直接的陈述，显示了立即采取的行动。

9.original: Under the same circumstances, Mr. P finds a good reason to put off writing the note,
9.b1: In the same situation, Mr. Passivationist finds a reason to delay writing the note,
9.cn: 在同样的情况下，被动派先生总能找到理由推迟写便条，
9.ai_notes: 'Under the same circumstances': in the same situation or condition.
9.remarks: 'Under the same circumstances': 在相同的情况或条件下。

10.ai_notes: 'gets written': a passive construction used to emphasize that the action failed to happen.
10.remarks: 'gets written': 一种被动语态结构，用于强调该动作未能发生。

11.original: The difference shows up in big things, too.
11.b1: This difference is also clear in important life decisions.
11.cn: 这种差异在大事上也表现得很明显。
11.remarks: 'shows up': 变得可见或显现出来。

12.original: Mr. A wants to go into business for himself.
12.b1: Mr. Activationist wants to start his own business.
12.cn: 行动派先生想自己创业。
12.ai_notes: 'go into business for himself': to start one's own company or become self-employed.
12.remarks: 'go into business for himself': 开始经营自己的公司或成为个体经营者。

13.ai_notes: 'He does': a simple, powerful phrase showing that he turned his idea into reality.
13.remarks: 'He does': 一个简单而有力的短语，显示他将想法变成了现实。

14.original: Mr. P also wants to go into business for himself, but he discovers just in the nick of time a good reason why he had better not.
14.b1: Mr. Passivationist also wants to start a business, but he finds a reason not to do it just in time.
14.cn: 被动派先生也想创业，但他总能在关键时刻找到理由不去行动。
14.ai_notes: 'in the nick of time': at the very last possible moment.
14.remarks: 'in the nick of time': 在最后可能的时刻。

15.original: Mr. A, age forty, decides he wants to take up a new line of work.
15.b1: At forty, Mr. Activationist decides to change his career.
15.cn: 四十岁的行动派先生决定换一份工作。
15.ai_notes: 'take up': to start a new activity, hobby, or job.
15.remarks: 'take up': 开始一项新的活动、爱好或工作。

16.ai_notes: 'He does': confirms that he successfully made the career change.
16.remarks: 'He does': 确认他成功地完成了职业转型。

17.original: The same idea occurs to Mr. P, but he debates himself out of doing anything about it.
17.b1: Mr. Passivationist has the same idea, but he talks himself out of it.
17.cn: 被动派先生也有同样的想法，但他最终说服自己放弃了。
17.ai_notes: 'debates himself out of': to use one's own logic or excuses to convince oneself not to do something.
17.remarks: 'debates himself out of': 利用自己的逻辑或借口来说服自己不要做某事。

18.original: The difference between Messer's activationist and passivationist shows through in all forms of behavior.
18.b1: The difference between these two types of people shows in everything they do.
18.cn: 行动派与被动派之间的差异体现在他们所有的行为中。
18.ai_notes: 'shows through': becomes evident or clearly visible in someone's actions.
18.remarks: 'shows through': 在某人的行为中变得明显或清晰可见。

19.original: Mr. A gets the things done he wants done, and as byproducts he gains confidence,
19.b1: Mr. Activationist finishes what he wants to do, and he gains confidence,
19.cn: 行动派先生完成了他想做的事，并因此获得了自信，
19.ai_notes: 'byproducts': secondary results or side effects that happen while doing something else.
19.remarks: 'byproducts': 在做某事时产生的次要结果或副作用。

20.original: a feeling of inner security, self-reliance, and more income.
20.b1: a sense of security, independence, and more money.
20.cn: 一种内在的安全感、独立性以及更多的收入。
20.ai_notes: 'self-reliance': the ability to depend on one's own powers and resources rather than others.
20.remarks: 'self-reliance': 依靠自身力量和资源而非他人的能力。

21.original: Mr. P doesn't get the things done he wants done because he won't act.
21.b1: Mr. Passivationist does not finish his goals because he does not take action.
21.cn: 被动派先生没能完成他的目标，因为他不愿采取行动。
21.ai_notes: 'won't act': indicates a refusal or lack of will to take the necessary steps.
21.remarks: 'won't act': 表示拒绝或缺乏采取必要步骤的意愿。

22.original: As byproducts, he loses confidence in himself, destroys his self-reliance, lives in mediocrity.
22.b1: As a result, he loses confidence, becomes dependent, and lives an ordinary life.
22.cn: 结果，他失去了自信，丧失了独立性，生活平庸。
22.ai_notes: 'mediocrity': the state of being average or below average; living a life without achieving one's potential.
22.remarks: 'mediocrity': 平庸的状态；指生活没有发挥出应有的潜力。

23.cn: 行动派先生会付诸行动。
23.ai_notes: 'does': emphasizes the act of doing rather than just thinking.
23.remarks: 'does': 强调行动本身，而非仅仅是思考。

24.original: Mr. Passivationist is going to do, but doesn't.
24.b1: Mr. Passivationist plans to act, but never does.
24.cn: 被动派先生打算行动，却从不行动。
24.ai_notes: 'is going to do': refers to an intention that never becomes an action.
24.remarks: 'is going to do': 指从未付诸行动的意图。

25.original: Everyone wants to be an Activationist.
25.b1: Everyone wants to be an Activationist.
25.cn: 每个人都想成为行动派。
25.ai_notes: 'Everyone wants to be': a common way to express a universal desire.
25.remarks: 'Everyone wants to be': 表达普遍愿望的一种常用方式。

26.original: So, let's get the action habit.
26.b1: So, let's build the habit of taking action.
26.cn: 所以，让我们养成行动的习惯吧。
26.ai_notes: 'get the action habit': to make taking action a regular, automatic part of your life.
26.remarks: 'get the action habit': 使采取行动成为你生活中一种常规的、自动的行为。

27.original: A lot of Passivationists got that way because they insisted on waiting until everything was 100% favorable before they took action.
27.b1: Many Passivationists are like this because they wait for perfect conditions before acting.
27.cn: 许多被动派之所以如此，是因为他们坚持要等到一切条件都完美时才采取行动。
27.ai_notes: 'got that way': became the kind of person they are now.
27.remarks: 'got that way': 成为了他们现在这样的人。

28.original: Perfection is highly desirable, but nothing man-made or man-designed is or can be absolutely perfect.
28.b1: Perfection is good, but nothing created by humans is ever perfect.
28.cn: 追求完美固然好，但人类创造的任何事物都不可能绝对完美。
28.ai_notes: 'man-made': anything created or built by humans.
28.remarks: 'man-made': 任何由人类创造或建造的东西。

29.original: So to wait for the perfect set of conditions is to wait forever.
29.b1: So, waiting for perfect conditions means waiting forever.
29.cn: 因此，等待完美的条件就等于永远在等待。
29.ai_notes: 'to wait forever': an idiom meaning that the event will never happen.
29.remarks: 'to wait forever': 一个习语，意为该事件永远不会发生。

30.original: Here are three case histories that show how three persons reacted to conditions.
30.b1: Here are three stories showing how three people reacted to different situations.
30.cn: 这里有三个案例，展示了三个人是如何应对不同情况的。
30.ai_notes: 'case histories': detailed accounts of a person's life or experiences used for study or analysis.
30.remarks: 'case histories': 用于研究或分析的个人生活或经历的详细记录。
